description_long: This object consists of two shovels, each featuring a robust wooden handle with a T-shaped grip at the end. The blades are made of dark, weathered metal, with one shovel having a square, flat blade and the other a reinforced, slightly curved blade with visible rivets and a partial metal collar extending up the handle. The metal surfaces show significant rust and discoloration, especially on the blade faces and the reinforcement plates. The handles are cylindrical, with a natural wood grain and some darkened areas near the metal fittings, suggesting frequent handling. The overall construction appears sturdy, with the shovels designed for heavy-duty use in urban or outdoor environments. No visible brand markings or logos are present on either shovel.
description: A pair of weathered shovels with wooden T-grip handles and rusted metal blades, one with a reinforced backplate. Both show signs of heavy use and aging.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing both shovels side by side, highlighting the flat and reinforced blade shapes and the T-grip handles.
description_view_1: Side profile view emphasizing the thinness of the blades and the cylindrical shape of the handles.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the underside of the blades, with the reinforcement and rivets on one shovel clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the slim profile of the shovels and the alignment of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: T-grip handle, cylindrical wood with a perpendicular crossbar at the end, natural finish, no visible ergonomic shaping.
attribute_blade_type: One square, flat blade with a reinforced backplate and rivets; one slightly curved, flat-edged blade, both made of metal.
attribute_wear_pattern: Significant rust and discoloration on the metal blades and reinforcement plates; darkened areas on the wooden handles near the metal fittings, indicating frequent use.
